then applied to Hono. The attack surface is generated from the specs —
always accurate, always complete.

Input/output schemas align with SAES `ActionSpec` conventions:
- `input`: Zod schema for the request body (`z.null()` for no body)
- `output`: Zod schema for the success response body
- `z.strictObject()` for inputs (reject unknown keys)

declarations.name: is_null_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is exactly `z.null()`.

Uses `instanceof` rather than runtime parsing to avoid false positives
from `z.nullable(z.string())` or similar schemas that accept null
but also accept other values.

declarations.name: is_void_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is exactly `z.void()`.

RPC action specs use `z.void()` to declare a parameterless method —
JSON-RPC 2.0 forbids `params: null` (params must be omitted or be a
Structured value), so `z.void()` is the correct schema for "no params"
and the dispatcher maps absent params to `undefined` for these specs.

declarations.name: is_strict_object_schema
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a schema is a strict object (`z.strictObject()`).

Strict objects set `catchall` to `ZodNever` to reject unknown keys.
Regular `z.object()` has `catchall: undefined` (strips unknown keys in Zod 4).

declarations.name: schema_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment:
Convert a Zod schema to a JSON-serializable representation for the surface.

Returns `null` for null schemas, JSON Schema for object schemas.

declarations.name: middleware_applies
declarations.doc_comment:
Check if a middleware path pattern applies to a route path.

Supports Hono-style patterns:
- `/api/*` matches `/api/anything`
- `/api/zap/*` matches `/api/zap/runs` but not `/api/account/login`
- Exact match: `/health` matches `/health`

declarations.name: merge_error_schemas
declarations.doc_comment:
Merge auto-derived, middleware, and explicit error schemas for a route spec.

Merge order: derived -> middleware -> explicit route errors.
Later layers override earlier ones for the same status code.
declarations.type_signature: (spec: { auth: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<...>>; params?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; query?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; rate_limit?: "both" | ... 2 more ... | undefined; errors?: Partial<...> | undefined; }, middleware_errors?: Partial<...> | ... 1 more ... | undefined): Partial<...> | null
declarations.return_description: merged error schemas, or `null` if empty

parameters.name: spec
parameters.type: { auth: { account: "none" | "optional" | "required"; actor: "none" | "optional" | "required"; roles?: readonly string[] | undefined; credential_types?: readonly string[] | undefined; }; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; params?: ZodObject<...> | undefined; query?: ZodObject<...> ...

parameters.name: middleware_errors
parameters.type: Partial<Record<number, ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>> | null | undefined
parameters.description: errors contributed by middleware whose path matches the route

modules.module_comment:
Shared pure helpers for schema introspection and middleware matching.

Used by both `http/route_spec.ts` (input validation) and `http/surface.ts`
(attack surface generation). Extracted to avoid circular dependencies
between routes and middleware.

declarations.name: filter_protected_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that require any form of authentication.

declarations.name: filter_public_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that are publicly accessible (no auth surface at all).

declarations.name: filter_role_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter all role-guarded routes (any role declared on `auth.roles`).

declarations.name: filter_authenticated_routes
declarations.doc_comment:
Filter routes that require basic authentication only — `account === 'required'`
with no role / credential gate.

declarations.name: filter_keeper_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that require keeper credentials (`daemon_token`).

declarations.name: filter_routes_for_role
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes whose `auth.roles` includes the named role.

declarations.name: RouteAuthCategory
declarations.doc_comment:
Categorize a `RouteAuth` into one of the legacy auth buckets.

Returns:
- `'none'` for fully public routes (account === 'none' && actor === 'none')
- `'keeper'` when `credential_types` includes `'daemon_token'`
- `'role:<name>'` for each role declared on `auth.roles` (multi-role specs
  are emitted multiple times; callers that need single-bucket grouping
  should pre-collapse)
- `'authenticated'` for `account === 'required'` without role / credential gate
- `'optional'` when either axis is `'optional'` and no other bucket fits
- `'other'` as a last-resort bucket for shapes that don't match above

declarations.name: routes_by_auth_type
declarations.doc_comment:
Group routes by auth category (see `RouteAuthCategory`). Multi-role specs
appear under each of their role buckets.

declarations.name: filter_routes_by_prefix
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes whose path starts with `prefix`.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_input
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null input schema.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_params
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null params schema.

declarations.name: filter_routes_with_query
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that have a non-null query schema.

declarations.name: filter_mutation_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that are mutations (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH).

declarations.name: filter_rate_limited_routes
declarations.doc_comment: Filter routes that declare rate limiting.

declarations.name: format_route_key
declarations.doc_comment: Format a route as `'METHOD /path'` (e.g. `'GET /health'`).

declarations.name: surface_auth_summary
declarations.doc_comment:
Summarize route auth distribution across the surface.

Categorical view over the four-axis flat record. Multi-role specs
contribute one count per role they admit.
declarations.type_signature: (surface: AppSurface): { none: number; authenticated: number; optional: number; role: Map<string, number>; keeper: number; other: number; }
declarations.return_type: { none: number; authenticated: number; optional: number; role: Map<string, number>; keeper: number; other: number; }
declarations.return_description: counts by auth category, with role counts broken out by role name

modules.module_comment:
Pure query functions over `AppSurface` data.

Usable in tests, the adversarial auth runner, and future surface explorer UI.
Replaces duplicated inline `.filter()` patterns.

Categorical filters (`filter_authenticated_routes`, `filter_role_routes`,
`filter_keeper_routes`) group the new flat-record `RouteAuth` shape into
the legacy categorical buckets (`'authenticated'`, `'role'`, `'keeper'`)
for adversarial test runners and the surface explorer. The buckets are
derived views over the four axes (`account`, `actor`, `roles`,
`credential_types`) — see `http/auth_shape.ts` for the canonical shape.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRoute
declarations.doc_comment: A route in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: is_mutation
properties.doc_comment: Whether this route mutates state (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH).

properties.name: transaction
properties.doc_comment: Whether this route's handler runs inside a database transaction.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key type declared on the route spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

properties.name: params_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the URL path params schema. `null` when no params.

properties.name: query_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the URL query params schema. `null` when no query schema.

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the request body schema. `null` for no-body routes.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the success response schema.

properties.name: error_schemas
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representations of error responses, keyed by HTTP status code. `null` when none.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceMiddleware
declarations.doc_comment: A middleware in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: error_schemas
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representations of error responses, keyed by HTTP status code. `null` when none.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceEnv
declarations.doc_comment: An env var in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: sensitivity
properties.doc_comment: Sensitivity level from `.meta({sensitivity})`. `null` when not sensitive.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceEvent
declarations.doc_comment: An SSE event in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRpcMethod
declarations.doc_comment: A method within an RPC endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the input schema. `null` for null-input methods.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema representation of the output schema.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key declared on the action spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceRpcEndpoint
declarations.doc_comment: An RPC endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

declarations.name: AppSurfaceWsMethod
declarations.doc_comment: A method within a WebSocket endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: kind
properties.doc_comment: `request_response` (inbound dispatch) or `remote_notification` (server → client).

properties.name: auth
properties.doc_comment:
Per-action auth shape. `null` for `remote_notification` (server →
client) — notifications have no inbound dispatch and therefore no
auth axis. `request_response` always carries a `RouteAuth`.

properties.name: input_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema of the input schema. `null` for nullary inputs.

properties.name: output_schema
properties.doc_comment: JSON Schema of the output schema.

properties.name: rate_limit_key
properties.doc_comment: Rate limit key declared on the action spec. `null` when not rate-limited.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceWsEndpoint
declarations.doc_comment: A WebSocket endpoint in the generated attack surface (JSON-serializable).

properties.name: allowed_origins
properties.doc_comment:
Upgrade-time origin allowlist, one entry per `WsEndpointSpec.allowed_origins`
regex stringified via `RegExp.prototype.toString()` (`'/<source>/<flags>'`).
Empty array when no origins were declared (any-origin); reviewers read this
as the exact pattern matched at the upgrade gate, not a wildcard
approximation. Reconstruct via `new RegExp(source, flags)` if needed.

properties.name: required_roles
properties.doc_comment:
Upgrade-time role gate — empty array when no `required_roles` was
declared (any-authenticated). Documents the coarse gate; per-action
`auth` on each method covers per-message authorization.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Assembly-time diagnostic collected during surface generation or server assembly.

declarations.name: AppSurface
declarations.doc_comment: Generated attack surface — JSON-serializable.

declarations.name: AppSurfaceSpec
declarations.doc_comment:
The surface bundled with the source specs that produced it.

`AppSurface` is JSON-serializable (snapshots, UI, startup logging) —
it's the observability layer, written to disk by `gro gen` for human
inspection + drift detection.

`AppSurfaceSpec` is runtime-only — tests, introspection, attack
surface assertions. Both in-process and cross-process tests construct
an `AppSurfaceSpec` in TS via `create_test_app_surface_spec` (or a
consumer equivalent); the cross-process-ness lives in the transport
+ per-test fixture, not the schema source.

declarations.name: RpcEndpointSpec
declarations.doc_comment: An RPC endpoint definition for surface generation.

declarations.name: GenerateAppSurfaceOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `generate_app_surface`.

properties.name: ws_endpoints
properties.doc_comment:
Mounted WS endpoints (the same array `create_app_server.ws_endpoints`
auto-mounts). Each entry's actions surface into
`AppSurface.ws_endpoints[i].methods` for attack-surface tests +
startup logging.

declarations.name: collect_middleware_errors
declarations.doc_comment: Collect error schemas from all middleware that applies to a route path.
declarations.type_signature: (middleware: MiddlewareSpec[], route_path: string): Partial<Record<number, ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>> | null
declarations.return_description: merged middleware error schemas, or `null` if none

declarations.name: env_schema_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment: Convert env schema to surface entries using `.meta()` metadata.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Zod object schema with `.meta()` on fields

declarations.name: events_to_surface
declarations.doc_comment: Convert SSE event specs to surface entries.

declarations.name: generate_app_surface
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate a JSON-serializable attack surface from middleware, route specs,
and optional env/event metadata.

declarations.name: create_app_surface_spec
declarations.doc_comment: Create an `AppSurfaceSpec` — the surface bundled with its source specs.

modules.module_comment:
App surface generation — JSON-serializable attack surface from route and middleware specs.

Pure schema helpers (`is_null_schema`, `schema_to_surface`, `middleware_applies`,
`merge_error_schemas`) live in `http/schema_helpers.ts`.

declarations.name: USERNAME_LENGTH_MIN
declarations.doc_comment: Minimum username length (must have start + middle + end characters).

declarations.name: USERNAME_LENGTH_MAX
declarations.doc_comment: Maximum username length (matches GitHub's limit).

declarations.name: USERNAME_PROVIDED_LENGTH_MAX
declarations.doc_comment: Maximum length for username input on login/lookup — more permissive than `USERNAME_LENGTH_MAX` for forward-compatibility if the creation limit is raised.

declarations.name: Username
declarations.doc_comment:
Username for account creation — starts with letter, alphanumeric/dash/underscore middle, ends with alphanumeric. No @ or . allowed.

Canonicalized to lowercase at parse time. The regex rejects whitespace
outright, so `.trim()` is unnecessary here. Storage is canonical across
every creation site (bootstrap, signup, admin-create, invite acceptance)
because the schema is the single source of truth — eliminates the
per-caller `trim().toLowerCase()` ritual and keeps the
`LOWER(username) = LOWER($1)` lookup contract simple.

declarations.name: UsernameProvided
declarations.doc_comment:
Username submitted for login or lookup — minimal validation for forward-compatibility if format rules change.

Canonicalized via `.trim().toLowerCase()` at parse time so login's
per-account rate-limit key and DB lookup see a uniform value
regardless of casing or surrounding whitespace. Mirrors the storage
canonicalization on `Username` so submission and storage agree.

declarations.name: Email
declarations.doc_comment:
Email validation. Lives here rather than `@fuzdev/fuz_util` because every
current consumer pairs it with `Username` (signup, invites, audit log) —
keeping the two together avoids a cross-package import for the
identity-primitive bundle. Promote to fuz_util if a non-identity consumer
surfaces.

modules.module_comment:
Reusable validator-schema primitives — `Username`, `UsernameProvided`,
`Email`. Lives at the top level (not inside `auth/` or `http/`) because
these shapes don't depend on or imply any domain — accounts hold a
username and email, but invites, password resets, future cell-sharing,
and other surfaces all reach for the same primitives without going
through auth.

Split out from `auth/account_schema.ts` so the auth module shrinks to
entity types + client-safe JSON shapes (its real responsibility) and
non-auth consumers can import these primitives without dragging the
auth domain along. Future cross-domain primitives (phone, url, slug)
land here too.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS
declarations.doc_comment:
Default tracked-key cap: bounds worst-case memory under key-enumeration
attacks (an attacker rotating source IPs cannot grow the backing map
indefinitely between cleanup ticks). Tuned to comfortably fit real
traffic for a single-instance deployment while capping memory at a few
MB in the worst case.

declarations.name: RateLimiterOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Configuration for a rate limiter instance.

properties.name: max_attempts
properties.doc_comment: Maximum allowed attempts within the window.

properties.name: window_ms
properties.doc_comment: Sliding window duration in milliseconds.

properties.name: cleanup_interval_ms
properties.doc_comment: Interval for pruning stale entries (0 disables the timer).

properties.name: max_keys
properties.doc_comment:
Maximum tracked keys. When exceeded, the least-recently-used key is
evicted — bounds memory under key-enumeration attacks. Default:
`DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS` (100_000). Pass `null` to disable the
cap (falls back to an unbounded `Map` — only recommended when the key
set is known to be closed, e.g. a per-account limiter keyed to a
bounded-size account table).

LRU trade-off: every `check` / `record` call marks the key as
most-recently-used, so keys under active attack stay fresh and won't
be evicted. A slow-burn attacker spread across many low-volume keys
can, however, drop out of the table and reset their budget — set
`max_keys` high enough to fit the expected legitimate key set and
this stays theoretical.

declarations.name: default_login_ip_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment: Default options for per-IP login rate limiting: 5 attempts per 15 minutes.

declarations.name: default_login_account_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment: Default options for per-account login rate limiting: 10 attempts per 30 minutes.

declarations.name: default_action_ip_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment:
Default options for per-IP action-dispatcher rate limiting: 600 attempts
per 15 minutes. Shared by the HTTP RPC and WebSocket action dispatchers
(one budget per action, not per transport). Permissive — catches runaway
scripts and egregious oracle probes, but well above human or normal
automation pace. Tighten downstream for stricter deployments.

declarations.name: default_action_account_rate_limit
declarations.doc_comment:
Default options for per-actor action-dispatcher rate limiting: 1200
attempts per 15 minutes. Shared by the HTTP RPC and WebSocket action
dispatchers. Permissive — sustained ~80/min is well above any human
admin workflow; an oracle probing 10k addresses still finishes in
~2 hours, slow enough to surface in audit. Tighten downstream.

declarations.name: RateLimitResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of a rate limit check or record operation.

properties.name: allowed
properties.doc_comment: Whether the request is allowed.

properties.name: remaining
properties.doc_comment: Remaining attempts before blocking.

properties.name: retry_after
properties.doc_comment: Seconds until the oldest active attempt expires (0 if allowed).

declarations.name: RateLimiter
declarations.doc_comment:
In-memory sliding window rate limiter.

Stores an array of timestamps per key. On `check`/`record`, timestamps
outside the window are pruned. `retry_after` reports seconds until the
oldest active timestamp expires.

The backing store is an `LruMap` when `options.max_keys` is a positive
number (default `DEFAULT_RATE_LIMITER_MAX_KEYS`) and a plain `Map` when
`max_keys` is `null`. The `LruMap` path bounds memory under
key-enumeration attack at the cost of a slight per-op overhead and the
LRU trade-off described on `RateLimiterOptions.max_keys`.

Parameters that accept `RateLimiter | null` (e.g. `ip_rate_limiter`,
`login_account_rate_limiter`) silently disable rate limiting when `null`
is passed — no checks are performed and all requests are allowed through.

members.name: check
members.doc_comment:
Check whether `key` is allowed without recording an attempt.

Prunes timestamps that fell outside the window as a side effect (and
removes the key entirely when none remain), so the backing map stays
bounded even under read-only traffic.

members.name: record
members.doc_comment: Record a failed attempt for `key` and return the updated result.

members.name: reset
members.doc_comment: Clear all attempts for `key` (e.g. after successful login).

members.name: cleanup
members.doc_comment: Remove entries whose timestamps are all outside the window.

members.name: dispose
members.doc_comment: Stop the cleanup timer. Safe to call multiple times.

declarations.name: create_rate_limiter
declarations.doc_comment: Create a `RateLimiter` with sensible defaults for per-IP login protection.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: override individual options; unset fields use `default_login_ip_rate_limit`

declarations.name: rate_limit_exceeded_response
declarations.doc_comment: Build a 429 rate-limit-exceeded JSON response with `Retry-After` header.
declarations.return_description: a 429 Response

parameters.name: retry_after
parameters.description: seconds until the client should retry

modules.module_comment:
In-memory sliding window rate limiter.

Tracks failed attempts per key (typically IP address) using a sliding
time window. No external dependencies — state resets on server restart.

declarations.name: AUDIT_LOG_CHANNEL
declarations.doc_comment: SSE channel the audit-log stream route publishes on.

declarations.name: disconnect_event_types
declarations.doc_comment:
Audit event types that trigger SSE stream disconnection — the union of
access-invalidation events. Over-closing a one-way admin feed is cheap (the
client reconnects if still authorized), so the SSE set is the full union.

`role_grant_revoke` requires the revoked role to match the guard's `required_role`
(or is skipped entirely when `required_role` is `null` — useful for streams
not gated by any specific role_grant). The WS half deliberately omits this
event (per-message re-authorization picks role changes up there); a one-way
SSE stream has no per-message recheck, so it must close here.
`session_revoke_all` / `token_revoke_all` / `password_change` / `logout` close
every stream for the target account.
`session_revoke` closes only the stream tied to the specific revoked session
(matched by the blake3 session hash in `event.metadata.session_id`) — closing
all of a user's streams for a single-session revoke would be over-aggressive.
The single `token_revoke` the WS half handles is omitted: an SSE stream is
opened under a cookie session, never an API token, so no stream is keyed by a
single token id.

declarations.name: create_sse_auth_guard
declarations.doc_comment:
Create an audit event handler that closes SSE streams on auth changes.

Closes streams when:
- `role_grant_revoke` fires for the `required_role` targeting a connected subscriber
- `session_revoke` targets the specific revoked session (session-hash-scoped)
- `session_revoke_all` / `token_revoke_all` / `password_change` / `logout`
  target a connected subscriber (account-wide)

The registry must use `account_id` as the identity key when subscribing
(passed as the third argument to `registry.subscribe()`).
declarations.type_signature: <T>(registry: SubscriberRegistry<T>, required_role: string | null, log: Logger): (event: AuditLogEvent) => void
declarations.return_description: an `on_audit_event` callback

parameters.name: required_role
parameters.description:
the role that grants access to the SSE endpoint,
or `null` to skip `role_grant_revoke` handling entirely (for streams not gated
by a specific role_grant)

declarations.name: AuditLogSse
declarations.doc_comment:
Convenience factory result for audit log SSE.

Satisfies `AuditLogRouteOptions['stream']` and provides the combined
`on_audit_event` callback (broadcast + guard).

properties.name: subscribe
properties.doc_comment: Subscribe function — pass as part of `stream` option to `create_audit_log_route_specs`.

properties.name: log
properties.doc_comment: Logger — pass as part of `stream` option to `create_audit_log_route_specs`.

properties.name: on_audit_event
properties.doc_comment: Combined broadcast + guard callback. Wired by `create_app_server`'s `audit_log_sse` option, or compose inside the consumer's `audit_factory` body.

properties.name: registry
properties.doc_comment: The underlying registry — exposed for subscriber count monitoring.

declarations.name: audit_log_event_specs
declarations.doc_comment:
SSE event specs for audit log events.

One spec per `AUDIT_EVENT_TYPES` entry, all sharing the `AuditLogEventJson` params schema.
Pass to `create_app_server`'s `event_specs` for surface generation and DEV validation.

declarations.name: AUDIT_LOG_SSE_MAX_PER_SCOPE
declarations.doc_comment:
Default max concurrent SSE subscribers per session scope for the audit log.

The audit log SSE subscribes with `scope = session_hash` and
`groups = [account_id]`. Only `scope` is capped — so this limits tabs
per session. An account's total streams across all sessions is bounded
transitively by `max_sessions × AUDIT_LOG_SSE_MAX_PER_SCOPE`. 10 tabs
per session is a comfortable ceiling for normal use; consumers raising
it above ~50 should consider server-side connection limits.

declarations.name: create_audit_log_sse
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a complete audit log SSE setup with broadcasting and auth guard.

Combines `SubscriberRegistry`, `create_sse_auth_guard`, and the broadcast
call into a single object. The result satisfies `AuditLogRouteOptions['stream']`
and provides the `on_audit_event` listener for the audit emitter's chain.

Most consumers pass `audit_log_sse: true` to `create_app_server` and never
touch this directly — the factory builds an `AuditLogSse`, appends
`audit_sse.on_audit_event` to `backend.deps.audit.on_event_chain`, and
exposes it via `AppServerContext.audit_sse`. Reach for the manual path
(compose inside `audit_factory` body, or
`audit.on_event_chain.push(audit_sse.on_audit_event)` post-assembly) only
when wiring outside `create_app_server`.
declarations.type_signature: (options: { role?: string | undefined; log: Logger; max_per_scope?: number | null | undefined; }): AuditLogSse
declarations.return_description: audit log SSE setup (stream options + `on_audit_event` + registry)

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { role?: string | undefined; log: Logger; max_per_scope?: number | null | undefined; }

modules.module_comment:
SSE auth guard and convenience factory for audit log SSE.

`create_sse_auth_guard` bridges audit events to `SubscriberRegistry.close_by_identity()`,
closing SSE streams when a subscriber's access is revoked (role revocation or
session invalidation).

`create_audit_log_sse` is a convenience factory that combines the registry,
guard, and broadcaster — making the secure path the easy path for consumers.

declarations.name: SseStream
declarations.doc_comment:
Generic SSE stream controller interface.

Transport-agnostic — works with any serializable type.

properties.name: send
properties.doc_comment: Send data to the client as a JSON SSE event.

properties.name: comment
properties.doc_comment: Send a comment (for keep-alive pings).

properties.name: close
properties.doc_comment: Close the stream.

properties.name: on_close
properties.doc_comment: Register a listener called when the stream closes (client disconnect or explicit close).

declarations.name: SseNotification
declarations.doc_comment:
Notification shape aligned with JSON-RPC 2.0.

Uses `{method, params}` to match the JSON-RPC notification format.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: Notification method name (e.g. 'run_created', 'host_updated').

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment: Method-specific payload.

declarations.name: create_sse_response
declarations.doc_comment:
Create an SSE response for a Hono context.

Wraps Hono's `streamSSE` to provide a `{response, stream}` API
compatible with `SubscriberRegistry` push-based broadcasting.
The callback suspends via a promise that resolves on client disconnect
or explicit `close()`, keeping the stream alive for external sends.

Uses `hono_stream.write()` directly (not `writeSSE`) to avoid
Hono's HTML callback resolution — keeps the same `data: JSON\n\n` format.
declarations.type_signature: <T = unknown>(c: Context<any, any, {}>, log: Logger): { response: Response; stream: SseStream<T>; }
declarations.return_description: object with the streaming `Response` and an `SseStream` controller

parameters.name: log
parameters.description: logger for serialization and `on_close` listener errors

declarations.name: SSE_CONNECTED_COMMENT
declarations.doc_comment: SSE comment sent on connect to flush headers through proxies. Exported for test assertions.

declarations.name: EventSpec
declarations.doc_comment: Spec for a push event — declares params schema, description, and channel.

properties.name: method
properties.doc_comment: Event method name, used as the JSON-RPC notification `method`.

properties.name: params
properties.doc_comment: Zod schema for the notification `params` payload.

properties.name: description
properties.doc_comment: Human-readable description for surface output and docs.

properties.name: channel
properties.doc_comment: Channel this event broadcasts on. Omit for cross-channel events.

declarations.name: create_validated_broadcaster
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a broadcaster that validates events in DEV mode.

In DEV: warns on unknown methods and invalid params.
In production: passes through with zero overhead.
declarations.type_signature: <T extends SseNotification>(broadcaster: { broadcast: (channel: string, data: T) => void; }, event_specs: EventSpec[], log: Logger): { broadcast: (channel: string, data: T) => void; }
declarations.return_description: validated broadcaster wrapper (passthrough in production)

parameters.name: log
parameters.description: logger used to emit DEV warnings on unknown methods or param mismatches

modules.module_comment:
SSE (Server-Sent Events) streaming utilities for Hono.

Provides generic helpers for creating SSE response streams
and a notification type aligned with JSON-RPC 2.0.

properties.name: channels
properties.doc_comment: Channels this subscriber listens to. `null` means all channels.

properties.name: scope
properties.doc_comment: Primary (capped) identity. `null` when none.

properties.name: groups
properties.doc_comment: Grouping identities for `close_by_identity`. `null` when none.

declarations.name: SubscriberRegistryOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `SubscriberRegistry`.

properties.name: max_per_scope
properties.doc_comment:
Max subscribers sharing a single `scope`. On subscribe, when the count
of subscribers with the same `scope` reaches this limit, the oldest
matching subscriber(s) are closed before the new one is added.
`null` (default) disables the cap. `groups` identities are never capped.

declarations.name: SubscribeOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for `SubscriberRegistry.subscribe`.

properties.name: channels
properties.doc_comment: Channels to subscribe to. Empty/absent = all channels.

properties.name: scope
properties.doc_comment:
Primary (capped) identity — e.g., session hash. Subject to
`max_per_scope` and matched by `close_by_identity`.

properties.name: groups
properties.doc_comment:
Grouping identities — e.g., account id. Matched by `close_by_identity`
but NOT subject to the cap. Use for coarse-targeted close.

declarations.name: SubscriberRegistry
declarations.doc_comment:
Generic subscriber registry with channel-based filtering and identity-keyed disconnection.

Subscribers connect with optional channel filters, a capped `scope`, and
uncapped `groups`. Broadcasts go to a specific channel and reach only
matching subscribers. `close_by_identity` force-closes all subscribers
whose `scope` or `groups` contain the given key — use for auth revocation.

members.name: subscribe
members.doc_comment: Add a subscriber.

parameters.name: stream
parameters.description: SSE stream to send data to

members.name: broadcast
members.doc_comment:
Broadcast data to all subscribers on a channel.

Subscribers with no channel filter receive all broadcasts.
Subscribers with a channel filter only receive matching broadcasts.

members.name: close_by_identity
members.doc_comment:
Force-close all subscribers whose `scope` or `groups` match the given key.

Closes each matching stream and removes the subscriber from the registry.
Use for auth revocation — when a user's permissions change, close their
SSE connections so they must reconnect and re-authenticate.

parameters.name: identity
parameters.description: the identity key to match (checked against `scope` and `groups`)

modules.module_comment:
Generic subscriber registry for broadcasting to SSE clients.

Supports channel-based filtering — subscribers connect with optional
channel filters, and broadcasts reach only matching subscribers.

Two identity slots enable both targeted disconnection and per-scope cap
enforcement:
- `scope` — a single capped identity (e.g., session hash). Subject to
  the per-scope cap and matched by `close_by_identity`. Use for the
  narrowest identity the subscriber belongs to.
- `groups` — any number of uncapped identities (e.g., account id).
  Matched by `close_by_identity` but not subject to any cap. Use for
  coarser scopes a stream should be reachable by.

The split keeps "tabs-per-session" cap semantics sane when a stream also
carries a broader identity for coarse close — the broader identity
doesn't cap across sessions.

declarations.name: create_deno_runtime
declarations.doc_comment:
Create a runtime backed by Deno APIs.

Returns an object satisfying all `*Deps` interfaces from `runtime/deps.ts`.
Pass to shared functions that accept `EnvDeps`, `FsReadDeps`, etc.
declarations.return_description: runtime implementation using Deno APIs

modules.module_comment:
Deno implementation of `RuntimeDeps`.

Provides `create_deno_runtime(args)` — a factory returning a `RuntimeDeps`
backed by Deno APIs. Only imported by Deno entry points (compiled binaries
for tx, zzz, etc.).

declarations.name: StatResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of a stat operation.

declarations.name: CommandResult

Result of executing a command.

`timed_out` is present only when `timeout_ms` was passed in `RunCommandOptions`
and the process was killed after exceeding the timeout. Callers that pass
`timeout_ms` should check this flag to distinguish timeout from exit-code failure.

properties.name: read_file

properties.name: read_text_from_offset
properties.doc_comment:
Read text starting from a byte offset. Throws if the file does not exist.

Returns `content`, `bytes_read`, and `file_size` so callers can detect
truncation (when `file_size < offset`) and tail incrementally without
re-reading the whole file.

properties.name: readdir

properties.name: run_command
properties.type_signature:
(
		cmd: string,
		args: Array<string>,
		options?: RunCommandOptions,
	) => Promise<CommandResult>
properties.doc_comment:
Run a command and return the result. Never throws — failures surface as
`success: false`.

`options.cwd` sets the child's working directory. `options.signal` aborts
the child when the signal fires. `options.timeout_ms` kills the child
after the given duration and returns `timed_out: true` on the result.

Shared Zod `.meta()` conventions for fuz_app schemas.

Cross-cutting metadata shape used by env schemas, auth input schemas,
surface generation, and test helpers.

Sensitivity level for a schema field.

Sensitivity levels for schema metadata.

Used by env schemas and auth input schemas to control masking/redaction

declarations.name: AppBackend
declarations.doc_comment:
Result of `create_app_backend()` — database metadata + deps bundle.

This is the initialized backend, not the HTTP server.
Pass it to `create_app_server()` to assemble the Hono app.

properties.name: migration_results
properties.doc_comment: Migration results from `create_app_backend` — auth migrations plus any consumer namespaces passed via `migration_namespaces`.

properties.name: close
properties.doc_comment: Close the database connection. Bound to the actual driver.

declarations.name: AuditFactory
declarations.doc_comment:
Callback that builds the bound `AuditEmitter` after the backend's pool
`Db` and `Logger` exist. Required on `CreateAppBackendOptions` so the
consumer owns subscriber-chain composition and `AuditLogConfig`
selection without the factory holding a default.

The factory is invoked exactly once during `create_app_backend`, after
`create_db` resolves and migrations run. The emitter it returns lands
on `AppDeps.audit` and is captured by every query/handler that reaches
`deps.audit.emit(...)`.

The canonical body is a one-liner over `create_audit_emitter`:

```ts
audit_factory: ({db, log}) => create_audit_emitter({
  db,
  log,
  on_audit_event,
  audit_log_config,
})
```

Returning an emitter built against a different `db` than the one passed
in would route audit writes to a different pool than handlers query —
the callback shape exists specifically to make that mistake structurally
impossible.

declarations.name: default_audit_factory
declarations.doc_comment:
Trivial `AuditFactory` for consumers that don't compose `on_audit_event`
or `audit_log_config`. Equivalent to
`({db, log}) => create_audit_emitter({db, log})` — exported so the
default case stays a single-symbol reference rather than five tokens
of boilerplate at every consumer.

Use the inline form when you need to thread `on_audit_event` /
`audit_log_config` / `emit_decorator`; the factory composes those
three fields itself so there's nothing this constant can pass through.

declarations.name: CreateAppBackendOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Input for `create_app_backend()`.

`keyring` is passed pre-validated — callers handle their own error reporting
(e.g., tx uses `runtime.exit(1)` on invalid keys).

properties.name: stat
properties.doc_comment: Get file/directory stats, or null if path doesn't exist.

properties.name: read_text_file
properties.doc_comment: Read a file as text.

properties.name: delete_file

properties.name: keyring

properties.name: password
properties.doc_comment: Password hashing implementation. Use `argon2_password_deps` in production.

properties.name: log
